response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency engineering at Sociomantic. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and the candidate's C++ experience amounts to a single six-month stint over a decade ago at National Instruments. The primary stack across the entire career is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no Rust mentioned at all. There is also no evidence of routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or the kind of map-data transformation work the role demands. The geospatial work is GIS data collection for NGOs, not production routing infrastructure.
strengths: Hands-on GIS/geospatial platform development (GISCollective, Green Map) shows genuine domain familiarity
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (millions of requests/second at Sociomantic using async I/O and performance databases)
Breadth across embedded, server, and distributed systems suggests adaptability and systems-level thinking
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust — the two required languages — in any meaningful capacity; C++ use was brief and over ten years ago
No routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance, or map-data transformation experience; GIS work is data collection tooling, not routing infrastructure
Career trajectory is firmly web/fullstack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang); pivoting to a C++ routing-pipeline role would require rebuilding core competencies from scratch
